title: Top considerations for financial intermediaries 2023
relevant_snippets: Now, investors must face the linked challenges of high inflation, rising yields and market volatility. To put this in perspective, inflation across all 38 members of the OECD between 2010 and 2020 (before the pandemic) ranged between 0% and 3% 1 . Now it is running at over 10%. This, combined with synchronized interest rate hikes (put in place by major central banks in response to inflation), risks triggering a global recession. Ironically, whether this economic contraction is mild or deep depends, at least in part, on the unpredictable path of inflation and further central bank responses.
5. Proliferation of alternative products Raising awareness of wealth management product development
73% of respondents are either currently invested or are considering to invest in illiquid assets over the next 12 months.
82% of respondents said that client demand for ESG products has increased over the past 12 months. The main driver for this demand was societal sentiment.

A sharp rise in macroeconomic volatility ended a decade of strong performance for 60/40 portfolios.
Global Wealth Management Investment Survey findings
59% of respondents believe the biggest investment opportunity over the next three years is in diversifying portfolios away from traditional equities and bonds.
